_comment: WIN-DLG-002 -- Options dialog (cui two-pane Material target). Composition-PINNING contract
(WIN-SYS-003 Document Properties precedent, adapted for a TREE master-detail shell instead of a
NOTEBOOK icon-rail shell) over the real upstream cui/uiconfig/ui/optionsdialog.ui +
cui/source/options/treeopt.cxx + cui/inc/treeopt.hrc. All three layers are 100% static /
compile-time facts (never officecfg/.xcu runtime data).
Pinned fail-closed: (1) the dialog shell -- modal GtkDialog OptionsDialog, the GtkTreeView
'pages' bound to GtkTreeStore 'liststore1' (2 columns) with headers-visible=False and
enable-tree-lines=True, plus the searchEntry/searchEntry_regex_builder pair (already wired to
sfx2::RegexSearchController per regex-search-integrations.json 'options.settings' -- cited as a
satisfied dependency, NOT re-integrated here); (2) the real footer action-widget sequence
help(-11)/revert(101)/ok(-5)/cancel(-6) with ok has-default; (3) the ordered 12 top-level option
groups, each an AddGroup(...) call site in treeopt.cxx keyed to its SID_*_RES[0] resource array in
treeopt.hrc, verified in ascending source position with its module guard (nullptr = unconditional,
pSwMod/pScMod/pSdMod/pSmMod = module-gated); (4) the shared tree native parts (listnode/listnet/
windowbackground/frame) already implemented app-wide in definition.xml, cited read-only.
Documented drift recorded as an explicit carve-out (NOT silently omitted): the footer button box
has FIVE buttons -- help, revert, apply, ok, cancel -- but the <action-widgets> block lists only
FOUR (help/revert/ok/cancel). The 'apply' GtkButton (wired to ApplyHdl_Impl, same handler family
as OK) has no response code and is absent from <action-widgets>. The checker pins this exact
reality: apply must exist as a button AND must NOT appear in the action-widget id set.
Carved out spec-only (build-dependent, never promoted): the per-group leaf pages (~80 entries
across the 12 groups) deferred to a follow-up widening; the tree row-selection fill (@primary-
container per 8.2) which has no definition.xml part (GtkTreeView row selection is native-drawn
outside the file-widget part vocabulary); the density / adaptive tree->dropdown collapse below
560px; and the field-grid / floating-label content-pane treatment (design-only, no source yet).
Source evidence only: runtime_verified is false throughout -- no native build, options-dialog
pixels, or runtime interaction are claimed. Group visibility at runtime additionally depends on
lcl_isOptionHidden()/module-active pointers; this contract pins the SOURCE guard, never the
runtime outcome.

search_field.dependency: regex-search-integrations.json ('options.settings') -- already wired to sfx2::RegexSearchController, cited read-only, not re-integrated by this row.

apply_drift.note: Recorded carve-out: the Apply button exists in the button box but is not in <action-widgets> (no response code). Design 8.2 states a 4-button footer; the real .ui has this 5th button. Pinned so it can never be silently added to or removed from the action set.

modal_exclusion.note: The Options dialog collects input; it stays KeepModal and is never routed into the notification stack. Read-only cross-check, not re-registered.

leaf_page_enumeration_deferred.note: The ~80 per-group leaf pages (RID_SW_TP_..., SID_SC_TP_..., etc.) are real static source that could be pinned with more effort but are deferred to a follow-up widening of this registry; not enumerated here.

tree_row_selection_fill.note: The selected-row fill (@primary-container per 8.2) has no equivalent part in definition.xml; GtkTreeView row selection is native-drawn outside the file-widget part vocabulary, so whether Material's selected-row treatment is achievable without new native paint is a build-host question, not this checker's.

density_adaptive_width.note: The adaptive width collapse (tree -> page dropdown below 560px) and density selection are design-only; no source exists to pin build-free.

field_grid_floating_label.note: The field grid / floating-label / validation-message content-pane treatment described in 8.2 is 'specified here, not yet implemented' in the design chapter itself; no source exists to pin yet.
